methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DroneForge is a pre-seed stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $2.5M to date. The comparable universe for this stage consists of five private peers in the sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Atropos, though no public comparables exist in this narrow category.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation approach here. Using total raised ($2.5M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a typical pre-seed to next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $6M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the drones sector; while four peers operate in the same sector, none met the filters for stage proximity or valuation-tier similarity needed to support a robust median.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5M to $7M, with a median point of $6M. This range reflects the single triangulation method available and carries material uncertainty stemming from the absence of stage-matched peer data. Key risks to this estimate include execution risk inherent to pre-seed ventures, the nascent and unproven commercial dynamics of the autonomous drones market, and potential future dilution from follow-on financing. Refinement of this valuation would benefit from additional pre-seed or seed-stage comparables in the autonomous systems space.
